markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define read/write/index permissions for operator, local model, cloud model, validator, and system actors. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: DocumentPermissionBoundary leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentPermissionBoundary is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

evidence.finding: Permission boundary fails open to 'system' on missing x-hsk-actor-kind and trusts a free client header
evidence.fix: Missing header now resolves to least-privileged Unauthenticated kind (read-only, write+index denied); unknown kinds stay strict 400; KernelActor receipt binding is server-derived from the validated kind only
evidence.tests: knowledge_documents_api_tests: mt158_missing_actor_kind_is_least_privileged_never_system + mt158_cloud_model_cannot_write_and_bogus_kind_is_rejected (real PG, 2 passed); lib knowledge_document::permission 4 passed incl. least_privileged_default_cannot_write_or_index

findings.title: Permission boundary fails open to the most-privileged actor and trusts a client-supplied kind header
findings.evidence: api/knowledge_documents.rs:183 actor_kind defaults to system when x-hsk-actor-kind is absent; the matrix (permission.rs:120-126) grants system + operator + local_model full read/write/index. The enforced actor_kind comes solely from the x-hsk-actor-kind header with no server-side binding of actor-id to kind, so the cloud_model-cannot-write rule is bypassed by omitting the header (->system) or asserting operator/system.
findings.fix: Derive actor_kind from an authenticated session/principal, not a free header; on absence default to the LEAST-privileged kind; reject requests whose asserted kind != authenticated principal.

claim_mismatches.claim: server-enforced permission boundary AUTHORITY decision function not a UI hint
claim_mismatches.reality: the attribute it enforces against (actor_kind) is fully caller-controlled via header and fails open to system on omission; the decision function is server-side but its input is not authenticated

missing_tests: test: request with NO x-hsk-actor-kind header is treated as least-privileged, not system
test: a caller cannot escalate by asserting actor_kind != its authenticated identity

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-158 requires a server-side read/write/index permission boundary for operator/local-model/cloud-model/validator/system actors over rich documents, backed by PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code implements a real server-enforced permission matrix wired into every knowledge-documents endpoint, the prior adversarial-v2 fail-open-to-system hole is genuinely closed, and the boundary is proven against real PostgreSQL through the booted route stack.
findings: permission.rs:120-152 defines DocumentPermission::decide() with the full actor matrix (operator/local_model full; cloud_model read+index no-write; validator read-only; system full; unauthenticated read-only) returning a PermissionDecision with stable reason codes.

knowledge_documents.rs:273-281 DocContext::require() enforces the matrix server-side and returns a 403 forbidden on denial; it is called on every mutating/reading endpoint (write at lines 638,816,915,962,1227,1323,1424,1471,1523; index at 725,1018,1377,1446; read at 748,781,1098 etc.). actor_kind is stored in DocContext (line 220,265) derived in doc_context(), not re-supplied per call, so the gate cannot be bypassed.

Prior adversarial-v2 HIGH (fail-open to system on missing x-hsk-actor-kind) is genuinely fixed in commit 5674367c: knowledge_documents.rs:244-251 now maps an absent header to DocumentActorKind::least_privileged() = Unauthenticated (read-only; write+index denied) instead of "system", an unknown token is a strict 400 (permission.rs:42-55 from_wire returns None for unknown incl. "SYSTEM"/"root"), and KernelActor is server-derived from the validated kind (lines 252-262) so a caller cannot pick an arbitrary receipt actor.

Real-PG proof: tests/knowledge_documents_api_tests.rs boots the actual docs_api::routes stack over loopback (doc_server, lines 114-145) against a real isolated PostgreSQL schema via knowledge_pg_support.rs (DATABASE_URL/POSTGRES_TEST_URL, CREATE SCHEMA, PostgresDatabase::connect with real migrations). Test mt158_missing_actor_kind_is_least_privileged_never_system (196-284) asserts absent-header create/save/rename/index all 403 with reason unauthenticated_write_denied while read stays 200 AND the doc content/version are unchanged; mt158_cloud_model_cannot_write_and_bogus_kind_is_rejected (287-348) asserts cloud_model write/create 403 (cloud_model_write_denied) and bogus kinds (root/SYSTEM/model_adapter/admin) 400.

Durable backing is real: migration 0280_knowledge_document_identity.sql:38-63 adds owner_actor_kind with a DB CHECK constraint (closed vocabulary mirroring KernelActor) and a paired NULL-consistency check; permission unit tests (permission.rs:178-223) cover the matrix incl. least_privileged_default_cannot_write_or_index and strict from_wire rejection.

No deferred-live escapes or placeholders (todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/pending MT-/Unavailable) in the impl or permission files. Aside (non-blocking): MT contract still cites v02.192 spec anchors; section 2.3.13.11 exists and is consistent in current v02.194 — pure paperwork, not a code issue.
evidence_checked: MT-158.json (contract, prior adversarial_review_v2 findings + FIXED status)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
